page: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the n4a server family. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This configuration lacks local storage and does not include bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The hardware profile is structured for compute-heavy workloads that require dedicated CPU resources without a large memory footprint. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance type provides a cost-efficient allocation of compute power relative to memory capacity. It is designed for tasks such as web serving, CI/CD pipelines, and distributed compute jobs where local storage is not a primary requirement.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the n4a server family, configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. This resource allocation provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, offering 2.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage, integrated GPUs, or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed as a compute-optimized resource, it delivers dedicated processing power for workloads that prioritize CPU capacity over memory density. This profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for processing-heavy tasks that do not require massive memory arrays or local disk storage. It is suitable for workloads such as web serving, CI/CD pipelines, and general compute-bound applications.
